worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's token counter confirms Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x text tokens and up to 3x image tokens at the same per-token price — concrete cost data every builder should see before committing to it.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image cost is resolution-dependent, not universal — prevents misreading the headline finding.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick argues OpenAI publicly releasing o1 may have gifted competitors the reasoning paradigm — a sharp strategic framing relevant to anyone thinking about AI moat.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Real-world report of Opus 4.7 confidently sticking to wrong answers and forcing a rollback to 4.6 — directly relevant to product decisions about model upgrades.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Someone reverse-engineered Claude Code's 512K lines into a visual map of the agent loop, tools, and orchestration — useful for anyone building on or competing with it.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router that picks cheap vs. expensive models per request in <2ms, claiming 70% cost reduction for agent builders — worth evaluating for cost-sensitive pipelines.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The sharpest product strategy take in the feed: if your roadmap exceeds 30 days in the AI era, you're already behind — high signal for indie dev and AI product operators.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Argues every human system — finance, comms, time management — must be rebuilt from first principles for agents, not just retrofitted.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Anthropic's official 33-page Claude Skills guide surfaced here with 625K views — if you haven't seen it, this is the pointer.

voices.why: Doing the actual empirical work on Opus 4.7 token costs with a real tool — the only person in this feed producing ground-truth numbers rather than vibes.
Consistently punchy on agent-native product strategy and distribution thinking; two posts here are directly relevant to the operator's interests.
Provides historical strategic context on AI lab decisions that others don't — the o1 release counterfactual thread is a good example.

examples: @aakashgupta posting 30+ off-topic takes (tennis longevity, Dragon Ball, Wolf of Wall Street, artillery shells, Nolan film budgets)
@RoundtableSpace all-caps crypto/trading bot shilling (Claude running Polymarket bots, $34→$21K weather trading)
@kimmonismus personal travel update and bare-URL posts with zero context
